name: Ko Hana Distillers
tagline: Oahu's only rum distillery — Hawaiian agricole rum made from 34 heritage sugarcane varieties grown on-site.
hours: Tours and tastings daily; check schedule
bestTime: Reserve ahead on weekends; weekday tastings are more intimate
talkStory: Founded by Jason Brand, Ko Hana grows 34 heritage varieties of Hawaiian sugarcane (ko) on their Kunia farm -- varieties that were nearly extinct after plantation-era monoculture. They produce agricole-style rum using fresh-pressed cane juice rather than molasses, a method closer to how pre-contact Hawaiians used sugarcane. Each single-varietal rum tastes dramatically different. The tasting room tour explains centuries of Hawaiian agricultural history that most visitors never learn.
